title: Dedicated illumination element and display based flash illumination for image capture field of the disclosure
abstract: Systems and methods for illuminating a subject for image capture are described herein. A processor may determine an ambient color temperature of a subject. The processor may further determine a color temperature for outputting light from a display facing a first direction based on a light output characteristic for a dedicated illumination element facing the first direction, the determined ambient color temperature, and a desired color temperature of the subject. The processor may further illuminate the subject using both the dedicated illumination element and the display. The display may output light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display. The processor may further capture, using an image sensor facing the first direction, an image of the subject during the subject being illuminated using both the dedicated illumination element and the display.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A mobile device comprising: a memory; and a processor configured to: determine an ambient color temperature of a subject; determine a color temperature for outputting light from a display facing a first direction based on a light output characteristic for a dedicated illumination element facing the first direction, the determined ambient color temperature, and a desired color temperature of the subject; illuminate the subject using both the dedicated illumination element and the display, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display; and capture, using an image sensor facing the first direction, an image of the subject during the subject being illuminated using both the dedicated illumination element and the display. 2. The mobile device of claim 1, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display by outputting light approximately uniformly across an entire surface area of the display. 3. The mobile device of claim 1, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display by outputting light from a plurality of areas including outputting light from a first area of the display according to a first parameter and outputting light from a second area of the display according to a second parameter, wherein a combined light output from the plurality of areas is based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display. 4. The mobile device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to select the light output characteristic for the dedicated illumination element from a plurality of light output characteristics available for the dedicated illumination element. 5. The mobile device of claim 4, wherein the light output characteristic is at least one of a color temperature and luminance for the dedicated illumination element. 6. The mobile device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to determine a luminance for outputting light from the display based on the light output characteristic for the dedicated 

 illumination element, the determined ambient color temperature, and the desired color temperature of the subject. 7. The mobile device of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to cause display of a preview of the subject on the display prior to illuminating the subject, then illuminate the subject and capture the image of the subject, and subsequent to capturing the image of the subject, cause display of another preview of the subject on the display. 8. The mobile device of claim 1, wherein the desired color temperature for the subject is selectable by a user. 9. The mobile device of claim 1 , wherein the desired color temperature is the determined ambient color temperature. 10. A method for illuminating a subject for image capture, the method comprising: determining an ambient color temperature of a subject; determining a color temperature for outputting light from a display facing a first direction based on a light output characteristic for a dedicated illumination element facing the first direction, the determined ambient color temperature, and a desired color temperature of the subject; illuminating the subject using both the dedicated illumination element and the display, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display; and capturing, using an image sensor facing the first direction, an image of the subject during the subject being illuminated using both the dedicated illumination element and the display. 1 1. The method of claim 10, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display by outputting light approximately uniformly across an entire surface area of the display. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display by outputting light from a plurality of areas including outputting light from a first area of the display according to a first parameter and outputting light from a second area of the display according to a second parameter, wherein a combined light output from the plurality of areas is based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display. 13. The method of claim 10, further comprising selecting the light output characteristic for the dedicated illumination element from a plurality of light output characteristics available for the dedicated illumination element. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the light output characteristic is at least one of a color temperature and luminance for the dedicated illumination element. 15. The method of claim 10, further comprising determining a luminance for outputting light from the display based on the light output characteristic for the dedicated illumination element, the determined ambient color temperature, and the desired color temperature of the subject. 16. The method of claim 10, further comprising displaying a preview of the subject on the display prior to illuminating the subject, then illuminating the subject and capturing the image of the subject, and subsequent to capturing the image of the subject displaying another preview of the subject on the display. 17. The method of claim 10, wherein the desired color temperature for the subject is selectable by a user. 18. The method of claim 10, wherein the desired color temperature is the determined ambient color temperature. 19. A mobile device comprising: means for determining an ambient color temperature of a subject; means for determining a color temperature for outputting light from a display facing a first direction based on a light output characteristic for a dedicated illumination element facing the first direction, the determined ambient color temperature, and a desired color temperature of the subject; means for illuminating the subject using both the dedicated illumination element and the display, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display; and means for capturing, using an image sensor facing the first direction, an image of the subject during the subject being illuminated using both the dedicated illumination element and the display. 20. The mobile device of claim 19, further comprising means for selecting the light output characteristic for the dedicated illumination element from a plurality of light output characteristics available for the dedicated illumination element. 21. The mobile device of claim 19, further comprising means for determining a luminance for outputting light from the display based on the light output characteristic for the dedicated illumination element, the determined ambient color temperature, and the desired color temperature of the subject. 22. The mobile device of claim 19, further comprising means for displaying a preview of the subject on the display prior to illuminating the subject, then illuminating the subject and capturing the image of the subject, and subsequent to capturing the image of the subject displaying of another preview of the subject on the display. 23. The mobile device of claim 19, wherein the desired color temperature for the subject is selectable by a user. 24. The mobile device of claim 19, wherein the desired color temperature is the determined ambient color temperature. 25. A non-transitory computer readable medium having stored thereon instructions that, when executed, cause a processor of a device to perform a method for illuminating a subject for image capture, the method comprising: determining an ambient color temperature of a subject; determining a color temperature for outputting light from a display facing a first direction based on a light output characteristic for a dedicated illumination element facing the first direction, the determined ambient color temperature, and a desired color temperature of the subject; illuminating the subject using both the dedicated illumination element and the display, wherein the display outputs light based on the determined color temperature for outputting light from the display; and capturing, using an image sensor facing the first direction, an image of the subject during the subject being illuminated using both the dedicated illumination element and the display. 26. The computer readable medium of claim 25, wherein the method further comprises selecting the light output characteristic for the dedicated illumination element from a plurality of light output characteristics available for the dedicated illumination element. 27. The computer readable medium of claim 25, wherein the method further comprises determining a luminance for outputting light from the display based on the light output characteristic for the dedicated illumination element, the determined ambient color temperature, and the desired color temperature of the subject. 28. The computer readable medium of claim 25, wherein the method further comprises displaying a preview of the subject on the display prior to illuminating the subject, then illuminating the subject and capturing the image of the subject, and subsequent to capturing the image of the subject displaying of another preview of the subject on the display. 29. The computer readable medium of claim 25, wherein the desired color temperature for the subject is selectable by a user. 30. The computer readable medium of claim 25, wherein the desired color temperature is the determined ambient color temperature.
